question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TransactionTemplate.setReadOnly(true) behavior is database-driver-dependent (some drivers ignore it) to cause inconsistent freeze-status reads where the mirror node reports an account as frozen even after an unfreeze transaction has been committed, leading to permanent apparent freeze?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-user or per-account rate limiting in the gRPC server configuration to flood the server with subscriptions for a specific topic, causing the listener buffer to overflow and drop unfreeze event messages for a targeted account, making the freeze permanent?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NettyServerBuilder customizer does not configure TLS, allowing a man-in-the-middle attacker on the same network to intercept and drop unfreeze event gRPC messages, causing the client to never receive the unfreeze confirmation and treating the account as permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC RST_STREAM frame at a precisely timed moment during an unfreeze event delivery to cause the server to abort the stream, and if the client does not retry, the unfreeze notification is permanently lost and the account remains frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionsPerIp or global connection limits in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to open thousands of connections, each with 5 streaming calls, causing the applicationTaskExecutor to be fully consumed and unfreeze event processing to be permanently starved?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a gRPC call that causes the readOnly TransactionTemplate to execute a query that returns stale freeze status data (due to read-only snapshot isolation), causing the mirror node to incorrectly report an account as FROZEN after an unfreeze has occurred on-chain, leading to permanent apparent freeze for downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the applicationTaskExecutor is shared between all gRPC service handlers to submit a large number of slow gRPC requests that monopolize the thread pool, causing the unfreeze event notification handler to be starved and unfreeze events to be permanently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request timeout configuration in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to hold a gRPC call open indefinitely, consuming one of the 5 maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots and preventing the victim's unfreeze subscription from being established?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a deadlock between the readOnly TransactionTemplate (holding a shared database lock) and a write transaction attempting to update freeze status, where the deadlock is not properly handled and results in the unfreeze write being permanently rolled back, leaving the account frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of HTTP/2 stream concurrency limits (beyond maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection) in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to send a SETTINGS frame with a very high INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE, causing the server to allocate excessive memory per stream and crash, preventing unfreeze event delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection=5 is a low default to perform a targeted denial-of-service against a specific mirror node connection used by a token holder, blocking all 5 slots so the holder cannot receive unfreeze event confirmations and their funds remain frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the readOnly TransactionTemplate bean is used to check entity existence (checkTopicExists) and the read-only transaction's snapshot does not reflect a recently committed unfreeze, causing the service to incorrectly reject the unfreeze event as invalid and leaving the account permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication or authorization in the gRPC server configuration to subscribe to freeze/unfreeze event streams for any account, and by consuming those streams without reading, cause the server's listener buffer to overflow and drop unfreeze events for the targeted account?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC request with a very large number of metadata key-value pairs (no header size limit configured in the NettyServerBuilder customizer) to cause the server to spend excessive time parsing headers, blocking the applicationTaskExecutor thread and delaying unfreeze event processing indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxInboundMetadataSize configuration in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to send a gRPC request with oversized metadata, causing a server-side exception that aborts the unfreeze event stream and leaves the account permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate to trigger a long-running read that holds a database connection from the pool, and by opening many such concurrent gRPC requests, exhaust the connection pool so that the write transaction recording an unfreeze event cannot proceed, causing permanent freeze?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-connection memory limits in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to send a stream of large gRPC messages that exhaust JVM heap, triggering GC pauses or OOM that prevent unfreeze event messages from being delivered and leaving accounts permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NettyServerBuilder customizer does not configure maxConnectionAge to keep connections alive indefinitely, and by holding connections with all 5 call slots occupied, prevent the server from accepting new connections from legitimate users who need to receive unfreeze notifications?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the readOnly TransactionTemplate to be used in a context where a freeze-status cache is populated, and by triggering many concurrent read-only transactions, cause the cache to be populated with stale FROZEN status that is never invalidated, resulting in permanent apparent freeze for all downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of gRPC interceptors (no authentication interceptor configured in GrpcConfiguration) to impersonate a legitimate subscriber and consume unfreeze event notifications intended for the real subscriber, causing the real subscriber to never receive the unfreeze confirmation and treating the account as permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of stream-level flow control configuration in the NettyServerBuilder customizer to send a gRPC streaming request and then stop reading, causing the server's write buffer to fill up, blocking the applicationTaskExecutor thread and preventing other unfreeze event streams from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a gRPC call that causes the readOnly TransactionTemplate to execute inside a nested transaction context, where the outer transaction is a write transaction for freeze status, and the readOnly flag propagates incorrectly (due to PROPAGATION_REQUIRED default), causing the write to be treated as read-only and the unfreeze to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP-based rate limiting in the gRPC server configuration to send a high-frequency stream of gRPC requests that cause the applicationTaskExecutor queue to overflow, resulting in RejectedExecutionException for unfreeze event processing tasks and permanent freeze?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NettyServerBuilder customizer sets the executor to applicationTaskExecutor (a shared Spring task executor) to cause thread starvation by submitting many blocking gRPC calls, preventing the executor from processing unfreeze event delivery tasks and making the freeze permanent?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readOnly TransactionTemplate bean's singleton scope to cause a race condition where two concurrent gRPC requests both use the same TransactionTemplate instance, and one request's transaction context leaks into the other, causing a freeze-status write to be executed in a read-only context and permanently failing?
